page:
# Day 5: Zooplankton and Food Webs
## Focus
Planktonic animals, trophic transfer, and the biological pump.
## Learning Objectives
- Identify major roles zooplankton play in marine food webs. - Explain how energy moves between trophic levels. - Describe how the biological pump transfers carbon to depth.
## Core Topics
### Zooplankton Diversity
Zooplankton include larval forms, grazers, predators, and vertically migrating organisms that link surface production to deeper waters.
### Trophic Structure
Energy transfer between trophic levels is incomplete. That inefficiency shapes predator-prey relationships and limits how much biomass can be supported at higher levels.
### The Biological Pump
Carbon fixed near the surface can sink through fecal pellets, marine snow, and dead biomass, helping transport carbon into the deep ocean.
## Applied Activity
Build a five-step food chain beginning with phytoplankton and ending with a large marine predator. Mark where energy is lost and where carbon may be exported to depth.
## Midweek Checkpoint
Complete the midweek quiz after reviewing Days 1 through 5 and updating your lab notebook with the main concepts from Units 1 and 2.
